title: myrsine africana var. bifaria
intro: Myrsine africana var. bifaria, also known as the African boxwood, is a variety of flowering plant in the family Myrsinaceae. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Myrsine africana var. bifaria include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and potentially lowering blood pressure. It can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional herbal medicine in Africa and Asia.
